title: A Day at the Baths
description: Marcus visits the public baths and describes the noisy scenes he finds there.

latin_text.text: Marcus thermās intrāvit. multī cīvēs ibi aderant; thermae semper plēnae erant.
in palestrā iuvenēs sē exercēbant et lūdēbant. Marcus eōs spectāvit.
deinde Marcus in tepidārium cucurrit et in aquam calidam dēscendit.
subitō clamōrem audīvit. mercātor in thermīs bona sua vendēbat et magnā vōce
clāmābat. omnēs īrātī erant quod quiētem petēbant.
Marcus rīsit. 'thermae' inquit 'semper tumultuōsae sunt. sed mihi placent!'

model_translation.text: Marcus entered the baths. Many citizens were there; the baths were always full. In the exercise yard young men were exercising and playing games. Marcus watched them. Then Marcus ran into the warm room and got into the warm water.

Suddenly he heard a shout. A merchant was selling his goods in the baths and was shouting in a loud voice. Everyone was angry because they were looking for quiet. Marcus laughed. 'The baths,' he said, 'are always noisy. But I like them!'

key_phrases: was selling his goods in the baths
everyone was angry because they were looking for quiet
